name: api-hash-binding
description: Binds API inputs and outputs cryptographically using HMAC-SHA256, for weak hash-chain proofs of computation.
long_description: This circuit provides the weakest form of proof: HMAC-SHA256 cryptographic binding between API inputs and outputs. It is suitable for scenarios where full ZK proofs are not practical, but hash-chain integrity is desired. The circuit verifies that an API call was made with specific inputs and produced specific outputs, but does not prove the correctness of the computation itself.

private.name: api_request_body
private.description: Request body sent to the API (JSON string)

public.name: response_hash
public.description: SHA-256 hash of the API response

public.name: binding_valid
public.description: Whether the input-output binding is valid

author: VRL Core Contributors
security_notes: Provides hash-chain integrity only, not computational correctness. Weakest form of proof in the VRL ecosystem. Suitable for audit trails and non-critical applications. NOT suitable for regulated or safety-critical systems.
performance_notes: Constraint count: 2 (minimal). Proof generation time: ~30ms. Proof size: ~1KB. Very lightweight, suitable for high-volume applications.
